LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL MILLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE DECISIONS IN GONZALES V SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 189 CAL. APP. 3D 1542 AND HOMESTEAD V SUPERIOR COURT (1986) 179 CAL. APP. 3D 494 MAKE CLEAR THAT A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT MUST BE DENIED WHERE THE MOTION FAILS TO DISPOSE OF EACH OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION. SUCH A MOTION CANNOT BE CONVERTED TO A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. GIVEN THE COURT'S RULING, THERE IS NO NEED TO ADDRESS THE PARTIES' EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. JUDGE: PATRICK J. Off Calendar Sep-28-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Bruce Glassmer, Esq., and Thomas Kawaii,Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Thomas H. Lutge
Basil Plastiras, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Michael C. Miller

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Thomas Lutge's motion for an order compelling responses to deposition questions and appointing a discovery referee is denied. The motion is untimely pursuant to CCP 2024.020. When the Court granted the motion to continue the trial on June 20, 2007, the time for completion of discovery was reset in accordance with the new trial date of October 9, 2007. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MICHAEL MILLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE DECISIONS IN GONZALES V SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 189 CAL. APP. 3D 1542 AND HOMESTEAD V SUPERIOR COURT (1986) 179 CAL. APP. 3D 494 MAKE CLEAR THAT A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT MUST BE DENIED WHERE THE MOTION FAILS TO DISPOSE OF EACH OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION. SUCH A MOTION CANNOT BE CONVERTED TO A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. GIVEN THE COURT'S RULING, THERE IS NO NEED TO ADDRESS THE PARTIES' EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
